target.name: Monarch Tractor
target.founder: Praveen Penmetsa, Zachary Omohundro, Carlo Mondavi

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $137M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Tekever
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Monarch Tractor is a Series C autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $137M to date. The comparable universe consists of six private companies operating in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Percepto; no public comps exist in this narrow segment.

Given the early-stage maturity of drone and autonomous vehicle markets, only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $137M total raised as a proxy for implied post-money valuation, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to next-stage advancement, yielding $233M. A peer-median approach could not be executed due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds of three or more datapoints within the narrow Drones & Autonomous category.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $198M to $268M, with a median point of $233M. This range reflects typical Series C step-up dynamics absent direct peer anchoring. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market comparables to ground long-term return expectations, limited visibility into the five-peer private comp set's recent financing activity and growth trajectories, and the sector's capital-intensity and regulatory dependency. Additional diligence on near-term revenue trajectories and competitive positioning relative to named peers would be necessary to narrow this range materially.
